content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                                EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
926 J Street, Suite 532
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660

September 8, 1975

Mr. Robert E. Mann
Smith, Kurlander, Humphrey & Mann
1255 Post Street, Suite 915
San Francisco, California 94109

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-170

Dear Mr. Mann:

    Thank you for your letter of August 25, 1975, requesting an opinion with respect to the obligations under the Political Reform Act of the Society for Individual Rights. Since your request raises no substantial question involving interpretation of the Act, your request for a formal opinion is denied.

    If Mr. Raya's activities on behalf of S.I.R. truly are voluntary in nature, as your letter suggests, S.I.R. does not incur any obligations under the Political Reform Act. If, however, S.I.R. provides Mr. Raya in-kind services worth $250 or more in any one month, S.I.R. will incur reporting obligations under Government Code Section 86108(b).

    I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have any further questions, please contact Ted Prin, a member of the Commission staff.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman, for the
                                        Commission

DHL:gp

Request No. 75-170
Date Received 8-28-75
Response Due 9-11-75

SMITH, KURLANDER, HUMPHREY & MANN
ATTORNEYS AT LAW:
1655 POST STREET, SUITE 515
SAN FRANCISCO, CA. 94103
(415) 441-5262

CLARK L V. BEICHLER
(OF COUNSEL)

EVANDER C. SMITH
FRED L. KURLANDER
GRIFFITH L. HUMPHREY
ROBERT E. MANN

August 25, 1975

Ms. Nancy Salzman
Fair Political Practice Commission
926 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Salzman:

I represent the Society For Individual Rights
(S.I.R.), a non-profit California corporation with
offices in San Francisco.

Mr. George M. Paya has been representing S.I.R.
before the California Legislature. He is, in addition, a registered lobbyist employed by the Human
Rights Trust Fund. This fund is his sole employer.
S.I.R. pays Mr. Paya no salary, nor does S.I.R. reimburse him for any travel expenses, food or lodging.

S.I.R. does provide Mr. Paya with a mailing address and a small box into which his mail is deposited
for his later retrieval. On rare occasions, Mr.
Paya has used a typewriter in the S.I.R. office for
a few hours and has used the telephone for local
calls.

Under these circumstances, does S.I.R. have any
liability for reporting or otherwise under the regulations of Proposition Nine?

May I have an appropriate ruling on this question so that S.I.R. can fulfill whatever legal
responsibilities it has in this area?

Very truly yours,

ROBERT E. MANN

REM/js
